data.title: Crypto Attacks Shift Focus from Smart Contracts to Private Key Breaches
data.description: In the past decade (2016–2026), the cryptocurrency sector has experienced cumulative losses of $17.1 billion, involving 518 incidents. According to Odaily, over the past five years (2021–2026), losses amounted to approximately $15.2 billion across more than 450 incidents. In the last year alone (April 2025–April 2026), the sector saw losses of around $2.5 billion from over 140 incidents. Recent data indicates a shift in crypto attacks from exploiting smart contract vulnerabilities to targeting private key leaks and access control issues.

data.title: France's Unemployment Declines in First Quarter
data.description: France's unemployment figures for the first quarter showed a decrease of 1.2%, bringing the total number of unemployed individuals to 3.295 million. According to Jin10, this marks a positive shift in the country's employment landscape.

data.title: Chinese EV Prices Highlighted at Beijing Auto Show
data.description: The Beijing Auto Show has brought attention to the significantly lower prices of Chinese electric vehicles (EVs) compared to those in the United States. According to NS3.AI, Chinese EVs are priced as low as $6,560, while the average price of a new car in the U.S. stands at $51,456. Reuters has identified five best-selling Chinese EVs priced between $10,000 and $12,000, underscoring the substantial price difference between the two markets.

data.title: Galaxy Digital OTC linked address transferred over 20,000 ETH to exchange within 24 hours.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the Galaxy Digital OTC associated address (0x16F6d15381bEE4A25B25F0d1C4161b78f7F21Fde) transferred a total of 21,369 ETH to exchanges in the past 24 hours, with a total value of $49.3 million and an average deposit price of $2,307.34.

data.title: A whale withdrew 4,361 ETH from Kraken, worth $9.98 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale, after three months of inactivity, withdrew 4,361 ETH from Kraken five hours ago, worth approximately $9.98 million. Separately, a new address withdrew 2,000 ETH from Binance one hour ago, worth approximately $4.58 million.

data.title: Crypto hackers have stolen a total of $17.1 billion in assets over the past decade.
data.description: Over the past decade (2016–2026), cumulative losses amounted to $17.1 billion, involving 518 incidents; over the past five years (2021–2026), approximately $15.2 billion was lost, involving over 450 incidents; and over the past year (April 2025–April 2026), approximately $2.5 billion was lost, involving over 140 incidents. Recent losses indicate that cryptographic attacks have shifted from smart contract vulnerabilities to private key leaks and access control breaches. (Solid Intel)

data.title: Bank of Japan Signals Potential Policy Action Amid Yen Depreciation
data.description: The Bank of Japan has indicated that significant depreciation of the yen may lead to policy intervention, as foreign exchange movements increasingly impact prices. According to NS3.AI, the central bank plans to continue adjusting interest rates in response to economic, price, and financial conditions. Naomi Rokkusha noted that this shift implies a change in the central bank's policy reaction function.

data.title: Binance HODLer Airdrops Adds USD.AI (CHIP), a Permissionless AI Infrastructure Lending Protocol
data.description:
Key Takeaways
Binance has announced USD.AI (CHIP) as the 63rd project on its HODLer Airdrops program, with 25 million CHIP tokens allocated as airdrop rewardsEligible users are those who subscribed BNB to Simple Earn or On-Chain Yields products between April 13, 00:00 UTC and April 15, 23:59 UTCCHIP tokens are expected to be distributed to eligible users' Spot Accounts within 5 hours of the announcementUSD.AI is a permissionless lending protocol enabling GPU operators to tokenize hardware as collateral and access instant financingTotal token supply is 10 billion CHIP, with 2 billion (20%) entering circulating supply upon Binance listing
Binance has listed USD.AI (CHIP) as the 63rd project on its HODLer Airdrops program, rewarding BNB holders who subscribed to Simple Earn or On-Chain Yields products during a retroactive snapshot window in mid-April.
USD.AI is a permissionless lending protocol built to finance artificial intelligence infrastructure. The protocol allows GPU operators to tokenize their hardware as collateral and access financing instantaneously -- targeting the fast-growing demand for AI compute financing as GPU infrastructure costs continue to scale alongside model development.
Airdrop Details
A total of 25 million CHIP tokens have been allocated for the HODLer Airdrops distribution. Eligibility is based on BNB subscriptions to Simple Earn (Flexible and/or Locked) and On-Chain Yields products held between April 13, 00:00 UTC and April 15, 23:59 UTC. Qualifying users will receive CHIP directly to their Spot Accounts within 5 hours of the announcement.
Individual rewards are calculated proportionally based on average BNB holdings during the snapshot period, subject to a hard cap: no single user's BNB holding ratio can exceed 4% of the total average BNB pool. Holdings above that threshold are calculated at the 4% ceiling.
Token Details
CHIP has a total and maximum supply of 10 billion tokens. At the time of Binance listing, 2 billion CHIP -- representing 20% of total supply -- will enter circulation. The token is deployed on both Ethereum and Arbitrum at contract address 0x0C1c1C109FE34733fca54b82d7B46B75CFb71F6e. A full research report is expected to be published within 48 hours of this announcement.
How HODLer Airdrops Works
Binance's HODLer Airdrops program rewards BNB holders retroactively based on historical balance snapshots, without requiring ongoing action beyond subscribing BNB to eligible Earn products. Snapshots are taken multiple times per hour at random intervals, with averages used to calculate final reward allocations. Users subscribed to Simple Earn also qualify automatically for Launchpool and Megadrop rewards, while On-Chain Yields subscribers qualify for HODLer Airdrops and Launchpool rewards.

data.title: Whales Continue to Accumulate Ethereum, On-chain Data Shows
data.description:
Whales are actively purchasing Ethereum, according to recent on-chain data. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the address 0xE5eB, which had been inactive for three months, withdrew 4,361 ETH from a centralized exchange five hours ago.

Additionally, a newly created wallet address, 0xA605, withdrew 2,000 ETH from a centralized exchange one hour ago.

data.title: AI Revolutionizes User Interfaces, Wallets Take a Backseat
data.description: Artificial intelligence is transforming user interfaces, pushing traditional 'wallet' functions into the background. According to PANews, the integration of AI into various platforms is reshaping how users interact with digital services, making processes more intuitive and seamless. This shift is leading to a reduced emphasis on conventional wallet applications, as AI-driven solutions offer enhanced user experiences. The evolution of AI technology continues to redefine the landscape of digital interactions, prioritizing efficiency and user-friendly designs.

data.title: HYPE Price Decline Leads to Major Short Position
data.description:
On April 28, HYPE's price experienced a decline, briefly touching the $40 mark. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the trader known as 'Loracle' on Hyperliquid has successfully closed a short position with 5x leverage, now holding a position valued at $23.8 million. The average entry price for this position was $40.871, making it the largest short position on-chain for HYPE.

The address associated with this position belongs to an early contributor to the Hyperliquid ecosystem and is the founder of Hypurrfun. Previously, during HYPE's surge from $20, this address was heavily invested in long positions, becoming the second-largest long position on-chain for HYPE. However, on April 20, the strategy shifted to shorting the asset.

Address: 0x8def9f50456c6c4e37fa5d3d57f108ed23992dae

data.title: Institutions: The Bank of Japan's "policy response function" has changed; the yen's exchange rate will be key to interest rate hikes.
data.description: A sharp weakening of the yen may prompt the Bank of Japan (BOJ) to take action, as the BOJ stated in a report that it will continue to raise interest rates based on economic, price, and financial conditions. Naomi Mukkara, a strategist at Mitsubishi UFJ Morgan Stanley Securities, said this marks a shift in the BOJ's "policy response function." In the report, the BOJ stated that foreign exchange rates are increasingly likely to influence prices, and such volatility could affect underlying inflation by altering inflation expectations. The BOJ does not directly target the yen, but if a significant depreciation of the yen accelerates price increases, the central bank may adjust its policy. (Jinshi)

data.title: WTI crude oil prices rose as much as 3%, while Brent crude oil is currently up 2.3%.
data.description: Gate data shows that WTI crude oil prices rose as much as 3% at one point, while Brent crude oil is currently up 2.3%.

data.title: Kalshi CEO Predicts Growth in Prediction Markets for Risk Transfer
data.description: Kalshi CEO Tarek Mansour has projected that institutional use of prediction markets for risk transfer could expand into a $10–15 trillion market. According to NS3.AI, Mansour noted that the current market stands at approximately $1–1.5 trillion, characterized by its illiquidity and predominance in over-the-counter trading.

data.title: Sky Protocol Nears $0.088 with Potential Upside
data.description: Sky Protocol is currently trading close to $0.088, with chart analysis suggesting a potential rise to $0.095. According to NS3.AI, the bullish trend remains strong as long as the support level at $0.078 is maintained. This follows a 28% increase since its breakout in February.

data.title: ZachXBT Criticizes Worldcoin's Token Practices
data.description: Blockchain investigator ZachXBT responded on X to Elon Musk's criticism of Sam Altman, highlighting concerns about Altman's company, Worldcoin. According to Foresight News, ZachXBT pointed out that Worldcoin launched a predatory cryptocurrency token, WLD, with extremely low circulation, comparable to the practices of SBF/FTX. The company allegedly distributed small amounts of WLD tokens to individuals in low-income countries to collect their biometric data. This technology, intended to verify human identity, has inadvertently led to a black market for account verification. The token supply has expanded unsustainably, with insiders regularly trading tokens over-the-counter.

data.title: CEO of Norway's sovereign wealth fund: AI partially offsets energy inflation, helping the market absorb shocks.
data.description: Nicolai Tangen, CEO of Norway's sovereign wealth fund, said the arrival of the artificial intelligence era is offsetting some of the inflationary impact of soaring energy prices triggered by the Middle East wars, helping markets absorb the shock. "This is remarkable because, given the current situation in the Middle East, you would expect this to translate into higher energy prices, higher fuel prices, higher fertilizer prices," Tangen said in an interview on Tuesday. He noted, "To some extent, this has already begun to impact Asia. We expect it to transmit to Europe and the US through price increases. This is usually bad for markets. But the markets are indeed coping calmly." Tangen said he was surprised that there hadn't been any major disruptions in the financial markets. "Inflation is usually a factor that hits markets. That's something we have to watch closely. On the other hand, we have AI, and we believe it has a deflationary effect. I think that's exactly what the market is currently digesting." (Jinshi)

data.title: BIT: Strategy's price action has diverged somewhat from Bitcoin's; their relative performance warrants attention.
data.description: BIT reports that Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) currently holds approximately $62 billion worth of Bitcoin at a cumulative purchase cost. Even during the past two quarters of Bitcoin's consolidation phase, the company continued to raise funds through the capital markets and used those funds to increase its BTC holdings. Historically, Strategy's stock price has had a close correlation with Bitcoin's price. During bull markets, Strategy's stock price is more sensitive to Bitcoin price changes, exhibiting greater upward elasticity and often outperforming Bitcoin; however, when market momentum weakens, the stock price sometimes weakens earlier than Bitcoin. Currently, Strategy's and Bitcoin's price movements are diverging somewhat. If past price patterns remain valid, this divergence warrants continued monitoring. If their price movements converge again, it could indicate an improvement in Strategy's relative performance compared to Bitcoin, but this change still depends on the overall market environment.

data.title: Bitget will list PROS spot trading
data.description: Bitget will be listing Pharos (PROS) spot trading. Deposits are now open, and trading will open at 21:00 (UTC+8) today.

data.title: Crédit Agricole Predicts Strong Dollar Sell-Off Amid Portfolio Rebalancing
data.description: Crédit Agricole has indicated that its end-of-month pricing model suggests a significant sell-off of the U.S. dollar. According to ChainCatcher, the global stock market showed overall strength in April, while the dollar weakened in the foreign exchange market. The institution anticipates that end-of-month portfolio rebalancing flows are likely to lead to a broad sell-off of the dollar, with the strongest sell signal against the euro. It is expected that the euro could further appreciate against the dollar.
